title: Rime of the ancient mariner
description: A mariner stops a man on his way to a wedding. The mariner then relates to the man all the events of a long sea voyage, arousing in his listener feeling of impatience, fear, fascination and bemusement.The Rime of the Ancient Mariner was published in the collection Lyrical Ballads (1798), which contributed significantly to the advent of modern poetry and the beginnings of British Romance literature.
subjects: The rime of the ancient mariner (Coleridge)
Rime of the ancient mariner (Coleridge, Samuel Taylor)
